briefdescription.para: I2C error reasons.

detaileddescription.para: Values that correspond to events or errors responsible for stopping an I2C transfer.

memberdef.definition: typedef void(* i2c_callback_t) (const struct device *dev, int result, void *data)
memberdef.argsstring: )(const struct device *dev, int result, void *data)
memberdef.name: i2c_callback_t

briefdescription.para: I2C callback for asynchronous transfer requests.

parameterdescription.para: I2C device which is notifying of transfer completion or error
Result code of the transfer request. 0 is success, -errno for failure.
Transfer requester supplied data which is passed along to the callback.

memberdef.argsstring: )(struct i2c_target_config *config)
memberdef.name: i2c_target_write_requested_cb_t

briefdescription.para: Function called when a write to the device is initiated.

detaileddescription.para: This function is invoked by the controller when the bus completes a start condition for a write operation to the address associated with a particular device.
A success return shall cause the controller to ACK the next byte received. An error return shall cause the controller to NACK the next byte received.

parameterdescription.para: the configuration structure associated with the device to which the operation is addressed.

simplesect.para: 0 if the write is accepted, or a negative error code.

memberdef.definition: typedef int(* i2c_target_write_received_cb_t) (struct i2c_target_config *config, uint8_t val)
memberdef.argsstring: )(struct i2c_target_config *config, uint8_t val)
memberdef.name: i2c_target_write_received_cb_t

briefdescription.para: Function called when a write to the device is continued.

detaileddescription.para: This function is invoked by the controller when it completes reception of a byte of data in an ongoing write operation to the device.
A success return shall cause the controller to ACK the next byte received. An error return shall cause the controller to NACK the next byte received.

parameterdescription.para: the configuration structure associated with the device to which the operation is addressed.
the byte received by the controller.

simplesect.para: 0 if more data can be accepted, or a negative error code.

memberdef.argsstring: )(struct i2c_target_config *config, uint8_t *val)
memberdef.name: i2c_target_read_requested_cb_t

briefdescription.para: Function called when a read from the device is initiated.

detaileddescription.para: This function is invoked by the controller when the bus completes a start condition for a read operation from the address associated with a particular device.

The value returned in *val will be transmitted. A success return shall cause the controller to react to additional read operations. An error return shall cause the controller to ignore bus operations until a new start condition is received.

parameterdescription.para: the configuration structure associated with the device to which the operation is addressed.
pointer to storage for the first byte of data to return for the read request.

simplesect.para: 0 if more data can be requested, or a negative error code.

memberdef.argsstring: )(struct i2c_target_config *config, uint8_t *val)
memberdef.name: i2c_target_read_processed_cb_t

briefdescription.para: Function called when a read from the device is continued.

detaileddescription.para: This function is invoked by the controller when the bus is ready to provide additional data for a read operation from the address associated with the device device.

The value returned in *val will be transmitted. A success return shall cause the controller to react to additional read operations. An error return shall cause the controller to ignore bus operations until a new start condition is received.

parameterdescription.para: the configuration structure associated with the device to which the operation is addressed.
pointer to storage for the next byte of data to return for the read request.

simplesect.para: 0 if data has been provided, or a negative error code.

memberdef.definition: typedef void(* i2c_target_buf_write_received_cb_t) (struct i2c_target_config *config, uint8_t *ptr, uint32_t len)
memberdef.argsstring: )(struct i2c_target_config *config, uint8_t *ptr, uint32_t len)
memberdef.name: i2c_target_buf_write_received_cb_t

briefdescription.para: Function called when a write to the device is completed.

detaileddescription.para: This function is invoked by the controller when it completes reception of data from the source buffer to the destination buffer in an ongoing write operation to the device.

parameterdescription.para: the configuration structure associated with the device to which the operation is addressed.
pointer to the buffer that contains the data to be transferred.
the length of the data to be transferred.

memberdef.definition: typedef int(* i2c_target_buf_read_requested_cb_t) (struct i2c_target_config *config, uint8_t **ptr, uint32_t *len)
memberdef.argsstring: )(struct i2c_target_config *config, uint8_t **ptr, uint32_t *len)
memberdef.name: i2c_target_buf_read_requested_cb_t

briefdescription.para: Function called when a read from the device is initiated.

detaileddescription.para: This function is invoked by the controller when the bus is ready to provide additional data by buffer for a read operation from the address associated with the device.

The value returned in **ptr and *len will be transmitted. A success return shall cause the controller to react to additional read operations. An error return shall cause the controller to ignore bus operations until a new start condition is received.

parameterdescription.para: the configuration structure associated with the device to which the operation is addressed.
pointer to storage for the address of data buffer to return for the read request.
pointer to storage for the length of the data to be transferred for the read request.

simplesect.para: 0 if data has been provided, or a negative error code.

memberdef.argsstring: )(struct i2c_target_config *config)
memberdef.name: i2c_target_stop_cb_t

briefdescription.para: Function called when a stop condition is observed after a start condition addressed to a particular device.

detaileddescription.para: This function is invoked by the controller when the bus is ready to provide additional data for a read operation from the address associated with the device device. After the function returns the controller shall enter a state where it is ready to react to new start conditions.

parameterdescription.para: the configuration structure associated with the device to which the operation is addressed.

memberdef.definition: typedef void(* i2c_target_error_cb_t) (struct i2c_target_config *config, enum i2c_error_reason error_code)
memberdef.argsstring: )(struct i2c_target_config *config, enum i2c_error_reason error_code)
memberdef.name: i2c_target_error_cb_t

briefdescription.para: Function called when an error is detected on the I2C bus while acting as a target.

detaileddescription.para: This function is invoked by the controller when a bus error, arbitration lost, or other critical error is detected during a transaction addressed to this device.

parameterdescription.para: the configuration structure associated with the device to which the operation is addressed.
an integer code identifying the error type.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct i2c_dt_spec *spec)
memberdef.name: i2c_is_ready_dt

briefdescription.para: Validate that I2C bus is ready.

parameterdescription.para: I2C specification from devicetree
if the I2C bus is ready for use.
if the I2C bus is not ready for use.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct i2c_msg *msg)
memberdef.name: i2c_is_read_op

briefdescription.para: Check if the current message is a read operation.

parameterdescription.para: The message to check

simplesect.para: true if the I2C message is a read operation
false if the I2C message is a write operation

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct i2c_msg *msg)
memberdef.name: i2c_is_stop_op

briefdescription.para: Check if the current message includes a stop.

parameterdescription.para: The message to check

simplesect.para: true if the I2C message includes a stop
false if the I2C message includes a stop

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, const struct i2c_msg *msgs, uint8_t num_msgs, uint16_t addr, bool dump_read)
memberdef.name: i2c_dump_msgs_rw

briefdescription.para: Dump out an I2C message.

detaileddescription.para: Dumps out a list of I2C messages. For any that are writes (W), the data is displayed in hex. Setting dump_read will dump the data for read messages too, which only makes sense when called after the messages have been processed.
It looks something like this (with name "testing"):

parameterdescription.para: Target for the messages being sent. Its name will be printed in the log.
Array of messages to dump.
Number of messages to dump.
Address of the I2C target device.
Dump data from I2C reads, otherwise only writes have data dumped.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, const struct i2c_msg *msgs, uint8_t num_msgs, uint16_t addr)
memberdef.name: i2c_dump_msgs

briefdescription.para: Dump out an I2C message, before it is executed.

detaileddescription.para: This is equivalent to: i2c_dump_msgs_rw(dev, msgs, num_msgs, addr, false); The read messages' data isn't dumped.

parameterdescription.para: Target for the messages being sent. Its name will be printed in the log.
Array of messages to dump.
Number of messages to dump.
Address of the I2C target device.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, struct i2c_msg *msgs, uint8_t num_msgs)
memberdef.name: i2c_xfer_stats

briefdescription.para: Updates the i2c stats for i2c transfers.

parameterdescription.para: I2C device to update stats for
Array of struct i2c_msg
Number of i2c_msgs

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, uint32_t dev_config)
memberdef.name: i2c_configure

briefdescription.para: Configure operation of a host controller.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.
Bit-packed 32-bit value to the device runtime configuration for the I2C controller.
If successful.
General input / output error, failed to configure device.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct i2c_dt_spec *spec, uint32_t dev_config)
memberdef.name: i2c_configure_dt

briefdescription.para: Configure operation of a host controller.

detaileddescription.para:
This is equivalent to: i2c_configure(spec->bus, dev_config);
spec
I2C specification from devicetree.
dev_config
Bit-packed 32-bit value to the device runtime configuration for the I2C controller.
a value from i2c_configure()

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, uint32_t *dev_config)
memberdef.name: i2c_get_config

briefdescription.para: Get configuration of a host controller.

detaileddescription.para: This routine provides a way to get current configuration. It is allowed to call the function before i2c_configure, because some I2C ports can be configured during init process. However, if the I2C port is not configured, i2c_get_config returns an error.
i2c_get_config can return cached config or probe hardware, but it has to be up to date with current configuration.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.
Pointer to return bit-packed 32-bit value of the I2C controller configuration.
If successful.
General input / output error.
Configured I2C frequency is invalid.
If get config is not implemented

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, struct i2c_msg *msgs, uint8_t num_msgs, uint16_t addr)
memberdef.name: i2c_transfer

briefdescription.para: Perform data transfer to another I2C device in controller mode.

detaileddescription.para: This routine provides a generic interface to perform data transfer to another I2C device synchronously. Use i2c_read()/i2c_write() for simple read or write.
The array of message msgs must not be NULL. The number of message num_msgs may be zero,in which case no transfer occurs.

simplesect.para: Not all scatter/gather transactions can be supported by all drivers. As an example, a gather write (multiple consecutive i2c_msg buffers all configured for I2C_MSG_WRITE) may be packed into a single transaction by some drivers, but others may emit each fragment as a distinct write transaction, which will not produce the same behavior. See the documentation of struct i2c_msg for limitations on support for multi-message bus transactions.

The last message in the scatter/gather transaction implies a STOP whether or not it is explicitly set. This ensures the bus is in a good state for the next transaction which may be from a different call context.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for an I2C controller driver configured in controller mode.
Array of messages to transfer.
Number of messages to transfer.
Address of the I2C target device.
If successful.
General input / output error.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, struct i2c_msg *msgs, uint8_t num_msgs, uint16_t addr, i2c_callback_t cb, void *userdata)
memberdef.name: i2c_transfer_cb

briefdescription.para: Perform data transfer to another I2C device in controller mode.

detaileddescription.para: This routine provides a generic interface to perform data transfer to another I2C device asynchronously with a callback completion.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for an I2C controller driver configured in controller mode.
Array of messages to transfer, must live until callback completes.
Number of messages to transfer.
Address of the I2C target device.
Function pointer for completion callback.
Userdata passed to callback.
If successful.
General input / output error.
If transfer async is not implemented
If the device is temporarily busy doing another transfer

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct i2c_dt_spec *spec, struct i2c_msg *msgs, uint8_t num_msgs, i2c_callback_t cb, void *userdata)
memberdef.name: i2c_transfer_cb_dt

briefdescription.para: Perform data transfer to another I2C device in master mode asynchronously.

detaileddescription.para:
This is equivalent to: i2c_transfer_cb(spec->bus, msgs, num_msgs, spec->addr, cb, userdata);
spec
I2C specification from devicetree.
msgs
Array of messages to transfer.
num_msgs
Number of messages to transfer.
cb
Function pointer for completion callback.
userdata
Userdata passed to callback.
a value from i2c_transfer_cb()

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, struct i2c_msg *msgs, uint8_t num_msgs, uint16_t addr, const void *write_buf, size_t num_write, void *read_buf, size_t num_read, i2c_callback_t cb, void *userdata)
memberdef.name: i2c_write_read_cb

briefdescription.para: Write then read data from an I2C device asynchronously.

detaileddescription.para: This supports the common operation "this is what I want", "now give it to me" transaction pair through a combined write-then-read bus transaction but using i2c_transfer_cb. This helper function expects caller to pass a message pointer with 2 and only 2 size.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for an I2C controller driver configured in master mode.
Array of messages to transfer.
Number of messages to transfer.
Address of the I2C device
Pointer to the data to be written
Number of bytes to write
Pointer to storage for read data
Number of bytes to read
Function pointer for completion callback.
Userdata passed to callback.
if successful
negative on error.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct i2c_dt_spec *spec, struct i2c_msg *msgs, uint8_t num_msgs, const void *write_buf, size_t num_write, void *read_buf, size_t num_read, i2c_callback_t cb, void *userdata)
memberdef.name: i2c_write_read_cb_dt

briefdescription.para: Write then read data from an I2C device asynchronously.

detaileddescription.para:
This is equivalent to: i2c_write_read_cb(spec->bus, msgs, num_msgs, spec->addr, write_buf, num_write, read_buf, num_read);
spec
I2C specification from devicetree.
msgs
Array of messages to transfer.
num_msgs
Number of messages to transfer.
write_buf
Pointer to the data to be written
num_write
Number of bytes to write
read_buf
Pointer to storage for read data
num_read
Number of bytes to read
cb
Function pointer for completion callback.
userdata
Userdata passed to callback.
a value from i2c_write_read_cb()

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, struct i2c_msg *msgs, uint8_t num_msgs, uint16_t addr, struct k_poll_signal *sig)
memberdef.name: i2c_transfer_signal

briefdescription.para: Perform data transfer to another I2C device in controller mode.

detaileddescription.para: This routine provides a generic interface to perform data transfer to another I2C device asynchronously with a k_poll_signal completion.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for an I2C controller driver configured in controller mode.
Array of messages to transfer, must live until callback completes.
Number of messages to transfer.
Address of the I2C target device.
Signal to notify of transfer completion.
If successful.
General input / output error.
If transfer async is not implemented
If the device is temporarily busy doing another transfer

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, struct rtio_iodev_sqe *iodev_sqe)
memberdef.name: i2c_iodev_submit_fallback

briefdescription.para: Fallback submit implementation.

detaileddescription.para: This implementation will schedule a blocking I2C transaction on the bus via the RTIO work queue. It is only used if the I2C driver did not implement the iodev_submit function.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for an I2C controller driver.
Prepared submissions queue entry connected to an iodev defined by I2C_DT_IODEV_DEFINE.

memberdef.argsstring: (struct rtio_iodev_sqe *iodev_sqe)
memberdef.name: i2c_iodev_submit

briefdescription.para: Submit request(s) to an I2C device with RTIO.

parameterdescription.para: Prepared submissions queue entry connected to an iodev defined by I2C_DT_IODEV_DEFINE.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct rtio_iodev *i2c_iodev)
memberdef.name: i2c_is_ready_iodev

briefdescription.para: Validate that I2C bus is ready.

parameterdescription.para: I2C iodev defined with I2C_DT_IODEV_DEFINE
if the I2C bus is ready for use.
if the I2C bus is not ready for use.

memberdef.argsstring: (struct rtio *r, struct rtio_iodev *iodev, const struct i2c_msg *msgs, uint8_t num_msgs)
memberdef.name: i2c_rtio_copy

briefdescription.para: Copy the i2c_msgs into a set of RTIO requests.

parameterdescription.para: RTIO context
RTIO IODev to target for the submissions
Array of messages
Number of i2c msgs in array
Last submission in the queue added
Not enough memory in the context to copy the requests

memberdef.argsstring: (struct rtio *r, struct rtio_iodev *iodev, uint8_t reg_addr, uint8_t data)
memberdef.name: i2c_rtio_copy_reg_write_byte

briefdescription.para: Copy the register address and data to a SQE.

parameterdescription.para: RTIO context
RTIO IODev to target for the submissions
target register address
data to be written
Last submission in the queue added
Not enough memory in the context to copy the requests

memberdef.argsstring: (struct rtio *r, struct rtio_iodev *iodev, uint8_t start_addr, void *buf, size_t num_bytes)
memberdef.name: i2c_rtio_copy_reg_burst_read

briefdescription.para: acquire and configure a i2c burst read transmission

parameterdescription.para: RTIO context
RTIO IODev to target for the submissions
target register address
Memory pool that stores the retrieved data.
Number of bytes to read.
Last submission in the queue added
Not enough memory in the context to copy the requests

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct i2c_dt_spec *spec, struct i2c_msg *msgs, uint8_t num_msgs)
memberdef.name: i2c_transfer_dt

briefdescription.para: Perform data transfer to another I2C device in controller mode.

detaileddescription.para:
This is equivalent to: i2c_transfer(spec->bus, msgs, num_msgs, spec->addr);
spec
I2C specification from devicetree.
msgs
Array of messages to transfer.
num_msgs
Number of messages to transfer.
a value from i2c_transfer()

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev)
memberdef.name: i2c_recover_bus

briefdescription.para: Recover the I2C bus.

detaileddescription.para: Attempt to recover the I2C bus.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for an I2C controller driver configured in controller mode.
If successful
If bus is not clear after recovery attempt.
General input / output error.
If bus recovery is not implemented

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, struct i2c_target_config *cfg)
memberdef.name: i2c_target_register

briefdescription.para: Registers the provided config as Target device of a controller.

detaileddescription.para: Enable I2C target mode for the 'dev' I2C bus driver using the provided 'config' struct containing the functions and parameters to send bus events. The I2C target will be registered at the address provided as 'address' struct member. Addressing mode - 7 or 10 bit - depends on the 'flags' struct member. Any I2C bus events related to the target mode will be passed onto I2C target device driver via a set of callback functions provided in the 'callbacks' struct member.
Most of the existing hardware allows simultaneous support for controller and target mode. This is however not guaranteed.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for an I2C controller driver configured in target mode.
Config struct with functions and parameters used by the I2C driver to send bus events
Is successful
If parameters are invalid
General input / output error.
If target mode is not implemented

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, struct i2c_target_config *cfg)
memberdef.name: i2c_target_unregister

briefdescription.para: Unregisters the provided config as Target device.

detaileddescription.para: This routine disables I2C target mode for the 'dev' I2C bus driver using the provided 'config' struct containing the functions and parameters to send bus events.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for an I2C controller driver configured in target mode.
Config struct with functions and parameters used by the I2C driver to send bus events
Is successful
If parameters are invalid
If target mode is not implemented

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev)
memberdef.name: i2c_target_driver_register

briefdescription.para: Instructs the I2C Target device to register itself to the I2C Controller.

detaileddescription.para: This routine instructs the I2C Target device to register itself to the I2C Controller via its parent controller's i2c_target_register() API.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the I2C target device (not itself an I2C controller).
Is successful
If parameters are invalid
General input / output error.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev)
memberdef.name: i2c_target_driver_unregister

briefdescription.para: Instructs the I2C Target device to unregister itself from the I2C Controller.

detaileddescription.para: This routine instructs the I2C Target device to unregister itself from the I2C Controller via its parent controller's i2c_target_register() API.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the I2C target device (not itself an I2C controller).
Is successful
If parameters are invalid

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, const uint8_t *buf, uint32_t num_bytes, uint16_t addr)
memberdef.name: i2c_write

briefdescription.para: Write a set amount of data to an I2C device.

detaileddescription.para: This routine writes a set amount of data synchronously.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for an I2C controller driver configured in controller mode.
Memory pool from which the data is transferred.
Number of bytes to write.
Address to the target I2C device for writing.
If successful.
General input / output error.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct i2c_dt_spec *spec, const uint8_t *buf, uint32_t num_bytes)
memberdef.name: i2c_write_dt

briefdescription.para: Write a set amount of data to an I2C device.

detaileddescription.para:
This is equivalent to: i2c_write(spec->bus, buf, num_bytes, spec->addr);
spec
I2C specification from devicetree.
buf
Memory pool from which the data is transferred.
num_bytes
Number of bytes to write.
a value from i2c_write()

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, uint8_t *buf, uint32_t num_bytes, uint16_t addr)
memberdef.name: i2c_read

briefdescription.para: Read a set amount of data from an I2C device.

detaileddescription.para: This routine reads a set amount of data synchronously.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for an I2C controller driver configured in controller mode.
Memory pool that stores the retrieved data.
Number of bytes to read.
Address of the I2C device being read.
If successful.
General input / output error.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct i2c_dt_spec *spec, uint8_t *buf, uint32_t num_bytes)
memberdef.name: i2c_read_dt

briefdescription.para: Read a set amount of data from an I2C device.

detaileddescription.para:
This is equivalent to: i2c_read(spec->bus, buf, num_bytes, spec->addr);
spec
I2C specification from devicetree.
buf
Memory pool that stores the retrieved data.
num_bytes
Number of bytes to read.
a value from i2c_read()

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, uint16_t addr, const void *write_buf, size_t num_write, void *read_buf, size_t num_read)
memberdef.name: i2c_write_read

briefdescription.para: Write then read data from an I2C device.

detaileddescription.para: This supports the common operation "this is what I want", "now give it to me" transaction pair through a combined write-then-read bus transaction.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for an I2C controller driver configured in controller mode.
Address of the I2C device
Pointer to the data to be written
Number of bytes to write
Pointer to storage for read data
Number of bytes to read
if successful
on error.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct i2c_dt_spec *spec, const void *write_buf, size_t num_write, void *read_buf, size_t num_read)
memberdef.name: i2c_write_read_dt

briefdescription.para: Write then read data from an I2C device.

detaileddescription.para:
This is equivalent to: i2c_write_read(spec->bus, spec->addr, write_buf, num_write, read_buf, num_read);
spec
I2C specification from devicetree.
write_buf
Pointer to the data to be written
num_write
Number of bytes to write
read_buf
Pointer to storage for read data
num_read
Number of bytes to read
a value from i2c_write_read()

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, uint16_t dev_addr, uint8_t start_addr, uint8_t *buf, uint32_t num_bytes)
memberdef.name: i2c_burst_read

briefdescription.para: Read multiple bytes from an internal address of an I2C device.

detaileddescription.para: This routine reads multiple bytes from an internal address of an I2C device synchronously.
Instances of this may be replaced by i2c_write_read().

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for an I2C controller driver configured in controller mode.
Address of the I2C device for reading.
Internal address from which the data is being read.
Memory pool that stores the retrieved data.
Number of bytes being read.
If successful.
General input / output error.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct i2c_dt_spec *spec, uint8_t start_addr, uint8_t *buf, uint32_t num_bytes)
memberdef.name: i2c_burst_read_dt

briefdescription.para: Read multiple bytes from an internal address of an I2C device.

detaileddescription.para:
This is equivalent to: i2c_burst_read(spec->bus, spec->addr, start_addr, buf, num_bytes);
spec
I2C specification from devicetree.
start_addr
Internal address from which the data is being read.
buf
Memory pool that stores the retrieved data.
num_bytes
Number of bytes to read.
a value from i2c_burst_read()

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, uint16_t dev_addr, uint8_t start_addr, const uint8_t *buf, uint32_t num_bytes)
memberdef.name: i2c_burst_write

briefdescription.para: Write multiple bytes to an internal address of an I2C device.

detaileddescription.para: This routine writes multiple bytes to an internal address of an I2C device synchronously.

simplesect.para: The combined write synthesized by this API may not be supported on all I2C devices. Uses of this API may be made more portable by replacing them with calls to i2c_write() passing a buffer containing the combined address and data.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for an I2C controller driver configured in controller mode.
Address of the I2C device for writing.
Internal address to which the data is being written.
Memory pool from which the data is transferred.
Number of bytes being written.
If successful.
General input / output error.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct i2c_dt_spec *spec, uint8_t start_addr, const uint8_t *buf, uint32_t num_bytes)
memberdef.name: i2c_burst_write_dt

briefdescription.para: Write multiple bytes to an internal address of an I2C device.

detaileddescription.para:
This is equivalent to: i2c_burst_write(spec->bus, spec->addr, start_addr, buf, num_bytes);
spec
I2C specification from devicetree.
start_addr
Internal address to which the data is being written.
buf
Memory pool from which the data is transferred.
num_bytes
Number of bytes being written.
a value from i2c_burst_write()

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, uint16_t dev_addr, uint8_t reg_addr, uint8_t *value)
memberdef.name: i2c_reg_read_byte

briefdescription.para: Read internal register of an I2C device.

detaileddescription.para: This routine reads the value of an 8-bit internal register of an I2C device synchronously.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for an I2C controller driver configured in controller mode.
Address of the I2C device for reading.
Address of the internal register being read.
Memory pool that stores the retrieved register value.
If successful.
General input / output error.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct i2c_dt_spec *spec, uint8_t reg_addr, uint8_t *value)
memberdef.name: i2c_reg_read_byte_dt

briefdescription.para: Read internal register of an I2C device.

detaileddescription.para:
This is equivalent to: i2c_reg_read_byte(spec->bus, spec->addr, reg_addr, value);
spec
I2C specification from devicetree.
reg_addr
Address of the internal register being read.
value
Memory pool that stores the retrieved register value.
a value from i2c_reg_read_byte()

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, uint16_t dev_addr, uint8_t reg_addr, uint8_t value)
memberdef.name: i2c_reg_write_byte

briefdescription.para: Write internal register of an I2C device.

detaileddescription.para: This routine writes a value to an 8-bit internal register of an I2C device synchronously.

simplesect.para: This function internally combines the register and value into a single bus transaction.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for an I2C controller driver configured in controller mode.
Address of the I2C device for writing.
Address of the internal register being written.
Value to be written to internal register.
If successful.
General input / output error.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct i2c_dt_spec *spec, uint8_t reg_addr, uint8_t value)
memberdef.name: i2c_reg_write_byte_dt

briefdescription.para: Write internal register of an I2C device.

detaileddescription.para:
This is equivalent to: i2c_reg_write_byte(spec->bus, spec->addr, reg_addr, value);
spec
I2C specification from devicetree.
reg_addr
Address of the internal register being written.
value
Value to be written to internal register.
a value from i2c_reg_write_byte()

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, uint8_t dev_addr, uint8_t reg_addr, uint8_t mask, uint8_t value)
memberdef.name: i2c_reg_update_byte

briefdescription.para: Update internal register of an I2C device.

detaileddescription.para: This routine updates the value of a set of bits from an 8-bit internal register of an I2C device synchronously.

simplesect.para: If the calculated new register value matches the value that was read this function will not generate a write operation.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for an I2C controller driver configured in controller mode.
Address of the I2C device for updating.
Address of the internal register being updated.
Bitmask for updating internal register.
Value for updating internal register.
If successful.
General input / output error.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct i2c_dt_spec *spec, uint8_t reg_addr, uint8_t mask, uint8_t value)
memberdef.name: i2c_reg_update_byte_dt

briefdescription.para: Update internal register of an I2C device.

detaileddescription.para:
This is equivalent to: i2c_reg_update_byte(spec->bus, spec->addr, reg_addr, mask, value);
spec
I2C specification from devicetree.
reg_addr
Address of the internal register being updated.
mask
Bitmask for updating internal register.
value
Value for updating internal register.
a value from i2c_reg_update_byte()

briefdescription.para: I2C Standard Speed: 100 kHz.
I2C Fast Speed: 400 kHz.
I2C Fast Plus Speed: 1 MHz.
I2C High Speed: 3.4 MHz.
I2C Ultra Fast Speed: 5 MHz.
Device Tree specified speed.
Use 10-bit addressing.

detaileddescription.para: DEPRECATED - Use I2C_MSG_ADDR_10_BITS instead.

briefdescription.para: Peripheral to act as Controller.
Structure initializer for i2c_dt_spec from devicetree (on I3C bus)

detaileddescription.para: This helper macro expands to a static initializer for a struct i2c_dt_spec by reading the relevant bus and address data from the devicetree.

parameterdescription.para: Devicetree node identifier for the I2C device whose struct i2c_dt_spec to create an initializer for

briefdescription.para: Structure initializer for i2c_dt_spec from devicetree (on I2C bus)

detaileddescription.para: This helper macro expands to a static initializer for a struct i2c_dt_spec by reading the relevant bus and address data from the devicetree.

parameterdescription.para: Devicetree node identifier for the I2C device whose struct i2c_dt_spec to create an initializer for

briefdescription.para: Structure initializer for i2c_dt_spec from devicetree.

detaileddescription.para: This helper macro expands to a static initializer for a struct i2c_dt_spec by reading the relevant bus and address data from the devicetree.

parameterdescription.para: Devicetree node identifier for the I2C device whose struct i2c_dt_spec to create an initializer for

briefdescription.para: Structure initializer for i2c_dt_spec from devicetree instance.

detaileddescription.para: This is equivalent to I2C_DT_SPEC_GET(DT_DRV_INST(inst)).

parameterdescription.para: Devicetree instance number

briefdescription.para: Write message to I2C bus.
Read message from I2C bus.
Send STOP after this message.
RESTART I2C transaction for this message.

simplesect.para: Not all I2C drivers have or require explicit support for this feature. Some drivers require this be present on a read message that follows a write, or vice-versa. Some drivers will merge adjacent fragments into a single transaction using this flag; some will not.

briefdescription.para: Use 10-bit addressing for this message.

simplesect.para: Not all SoC I2C implementations support this feature.

briefdescription.para: Target device responds to 10-bit addressing.

memberdef.name: I2C_DEVICE_DT_DEINIT_DEFINE
memberdef.initializer: Z_I2C_DEVICE_STATE_DEFINE(Z_DEVICE_DT_DEV_ID(node_id)); \ Z_I2C_INIT_FN(Z_DEVICE_DT_DEV_ID(node_id), init_fn) \ Z_DEVICE_DEFINE(node_id, Z_DEVICE_DT_DEV_ID(node_id), \ DEVICE_DT_NAME(node_id), \ &UTIL_CAT(Z_DEVICE_DT_DEV_ID(node_id), _init), \ deinit_fn, Z_DEVICE_DT_FLAGS(node_id), pm, data,\ config, level, prio, api, \ &(Z_DEVICE_STATE_NAME(Z_DEVICE_DT_DEV_ID(node_id)).devstate), \ __VA_ARGS__)

briefdescription.para: Like DEVICE_DT_DEINIT_DEFINE() with I2C specifics.

detaileddescription.para: Defines a device which implements the I2C API. May generate a custom device_state container struct and init_fn wrapper when needed depending on I2C CONFIG_I2C_STATS.

parameterdescription.para: The devicetree node identifier.
Name of the init function of the driver. Can be NULL.
Name of the deinit function of the driver. Can be NULL.
PM device resources reference (NULL if device does not use PM).
Pointer to the device's private data.
The address to the structure containing the configuration information for this instance of the driver.
The initialization level. See SYS_INIT() for details.
Priority within the selected initialization level. See SYS_INIT() for details.
Provides an initial pointer to the API function struct used by the driver. Can be NULL.

memberdef.name: I2C_DEVICE_DT_DEFINE
memberdef.initializer: I2C_DEVICE_DT_DEINIT_DEFINE(node_id, init_fn, NULL, pm, data, \ config, level, prio, api, \ __VA_ARGS__)

briefdescription.para: Like I2C_DEVICE_DT_DEINIT_DEFINE() but without deinit_fn.
Like I2C_DEVICE_DT_DEINIT_DEFINE() for an instance of a DT_DRV_COMPAT compatible.

parameterdescription.para: instance number. This is replaced by DT_DRV_COMPAT(inst) in the call to I2C_DEVICE_DT_DEINIT_DEFINE().
other parameters as expected by I2C_DEVICE_DT_DEINIT_DEFINE().

briefdescription.para: Like I2C_DEVICE_DT_DEFINE() for an instance of a DT_DRV_COMPAT compatible.

parameterdescription.para: instance number. This is replaced by DT_DRV_COMPAT(inst) in the call to I2C_DEVICE_DT_DEFINE().
other parameters as expected by I2C_DEVICE_DT_DEFINE().

briefdescription.para: Define an iodev for a given dt node on the bus.

detaileddescription.para: These do not need to be shared globally but doing so will save a small amount of memory.

parameterdescription.para: Symbolic name of the iodev to define
Devicetree node identifier

briefdescription.para: Define an iodev for a devicetree instance on the bus.

detaileddescription.para: This is equivalent to I2C_DT_IODEV_DEFINE(name, DT_DRV_INST(inst)).

parameterdescription.para: Symbolic name of the iodev to define
Devicetree instance number

memberdef.name: I2C_IODEV_DEFINE
memberdef.initializer: const struct i2c_dt_spec I2C_CAT2(_i2c_dt_spec_, name) = { \ .bus = DEVICE_DT_GET(_bus), \ .addr = _addr, \ }; \ RTIO_IODEV_DEFINE(name, &i2c_iodev_api, (void *)&I2C_CAT2(_i2c_dt_spec_, name))

briefdescription.para: Define an iodev for a given i2c device on a bus.

detaileddescription.para: These do not need to be shared globally but doing so will save a small amount of memory.

parameterdescription.para: Symbolic name of the iodev to define
Node ID for I2C bus
I2C target address

briefdescription.para: Interfaces for Inter-Integrated Circuit (I2C) controllers.
